evidence_quotes.quote: subscription service renewal for online services should be opt-in by default, rather than opt-out, and subscriptions should never run indefinitely.
The subscription should automatically expire after some time if no explicit consent is acquired from the consumer.
Ideally the consumer would have the option to configure how often they want to to be reminded to renew each service, e.g. every 1 month, every 3 months, every 6 months, every year.
The default value should always be equal to the subscription period (so that it automatically expires after the first period if not renewed) and the maximum period should be 1 year.
Billing itself would still take place every month (or however long the subscription period is), as is happening now.
If I don't explicitly renew, the subscription will automatically expire after the end of the third month.

paragraphs.text: I strongly believe that subscription service renewal for online services should be opt-in by default, rather than opt-out, and subscriptions should never run indefinitely. The subscription should automatically expire after some time if no explicit consent is acquired from the consumer. Ideally the consumer would have the option to configure how often they want to to be reminded to renew each service, e.g. every 1 month, every 3 months, every 6 months, every year. The default value should always be equal to the subscription period (so that it automatically expires after the first period if not renewed) and the maximum period should be 1 year. Billing itself would still take place every month (or however long the subscription period is), as is happening now. For example: I subscribe to a service that is 8 per month. I choose to be reminded to renew every three months. Halfway through the third month, I should get an e-mail to remind me to renew my subscription. If I don't explicitly renew, the subscription will automatically expire after the end of the third month. Crucially, this e-mail should require explicit consent from the consumer. No dark patterns like" click here if you __don't__ want to renew". I believe this way there is a fair middle ground for everyone involved. Consumers who don't want to be bothered with e-mails every month only have to give consent once a year, while others can configure it from themselves as they see fit.
